summary_one_line: Input must contain a structurally valid GPOS table (literal 'GPOS' FOURCC tag) so num_features>0 and the feature loop is entered.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must carry the 4-byte ASCII table tag 'GPOS' (0x47 0x50 0x4f 0x53) at the sfnt table-directory entry so the GPOS table is located and gpos.get_feature_count() returns num_features>0; the loop at L1680 then takes its true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed places the literal bytes 'GPOS' at offsets 0x0c-0x0f, giving a valid GPOS table directory entry; the branch true-side fires (W shows T=129) and the body at L1681/L1682 is reached only in W.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds have no 'GPOS' tag at 0x0c-0x0f (they hold values like 47/01/00/2c, 50/20/00, etc.), so no GPOS table is located, num_features stays 0, and the loop true side is never taken (L shows T=0 F=20).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the intercepted tag comparison (the HB_TAG sfnt-table-tag compare) from the CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes the 'GPOS' operand into the input at the matching offset, planting the exact 4-byte tag that naive/value_profile must find by blind havoc.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the feature loop gated on num_features, derived from gpos.get_feature_count().
A HB_TAG sfnt table tag drives table lookup in this function.
Winner seeds carry the literal ASCII 'GPOS' tag at offsets 0x0c-0x0f.
At 0x0f winner is uniformly 'S' (0x53) while loser is not.
Winner-resolving seed bytes show 'GPOS' at offset 0x0c.
The blocker loop true side fires only in winner seeds (T=129 in W, T=0 in L).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L1680  T=129 F=20  T=0 F=20  for (unsigned int i = 0; i < num_features; i++)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Loser-blocking seeds lack a GPOS tag and are short/unstructured.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a recorded CMP operand into matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the I2S delta separates the vp pair.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5567 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed 0119dc42c7db21ec` and observed one I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only) at depth 17 of the 18-level ancestor chain; the tool's trailing 'I2S-floor signal' line reports '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 17', direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in the winning seed's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose GPOS-table-tag compare is removed (table located by index, not by an intercepted 4-byte tag CMP) showing cmplog/vpc NOT outpacing naive/value_profile at entering the feature loop.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff shows 'GPOS' at a fixed offset 0x0c-0x0f in all sampled winners, but I infer (rather than directly observe) that the sfnt table-directory tag CMP is the specific comparison I2SRandReplace solves.
